title: There Is No God, the Wicked Sayeth by Arthur Hugh Clough — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
"There is no God," the wicked saith,
"And truly it's a blessing,
For what He might have done with us
It's better only guessing."

"There is no God," a youngster thinks,
"or really, if there may be,
He surely did not mean a man
meaning: "There Is No God, the Wicked Sayeth" by Arthur Hugh Clough is a deep poem consisting of 39 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with ""There is no God," the wicked saith, "And truly it's a blessing,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Arthur Hugh Clough's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
